title: NIS2 Cross-Border Incident Coordination Task Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 Art.23(3) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.23(3) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.34: NIS2 Cross-Border Incident Coordination Task Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.34' running on index=itsm (child tasks per country), nis2_cross_border_incident_register.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| inputlookup nis2_cross_border_incident_register.csv
| join incident_id [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_task" short_description="*CSIRT*" earliest=-90d
    | stats dc(short_description) as tasks_created sum(eval(case(state!="Closed",1,0))) as open_tasks by parent_incident
    | rename parent_incident as incident_id
]
| eval expected_tasks=affected_member_states*2
| where open_tasks > 0 OR tasks_created < expected_tasks
| table incident_id, affected_member_states, tasks_created, open_tasks, expected_tasks
description: When incidents affect multiple Member States, parallel coordination tasks multiply. This use case tracks per-country CSIRT notification tickets, language-specific attachments, and follow-up deadlines — complementing UC-22.2.19's technical cross-border detection.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:sc_task. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) When cross-border flag is set, auto-spawn tasks per Member State template; (2) track filing confirmations as attachments; (3) alert incident commander on open_tasks; (4) post-incident merge lessons into UC-22.2.23; (5) legal owns register accuracy. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:sc_task`; Lookups: `nis2_cross_border_incident_register`, `nis2_cross_border_incident_register.csv`.
visualization: Table (open coordination tasks), Map (Member States), Gantt-style panel (optional), Single value (incidents with open tasks). Key fields: `affected_member_states`, `expected_tasks`, `incident_id`, `open_tasks`, `tasks_created`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management by All_Ticket_Management.status, All_Ticket_Management.priority, All_Ticket_Management.category | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` (child tasks per country), `nis2_cross_border_incident_register.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) When cross-border flag is set, auto-spawn tasks per Member State template; (2) track filing confirmations as attachments; (3) alert incident commander on open_tasks; (4) post-incident merge lessons into UC-22.2.23; (5) legal owns register accuracy.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_cross_border_incident_register.csv
| join incident_id [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_task" short_description="*CSIRT*" earliest=-90d
    | stats dc(short_description) as tasks_created sum(eval(case(state!="Closed",1,0))) as open_tasks by parent_incident
    | rename parent_incident as incident_id
]
| eval expected_tasks=affected_member_states*2
| where open_tasks > 0 OR tasks_created < expected_tasks
| table incident_id, affected_member_states, tasks_created, open_tasks, expected_tasks
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Cross-Border Incident Coordination Task Tracking (Art. 23(3))** — When incidents affect multiple Member States, parallel coordination tasks multiply. This use case tracks per-country CSIRT notification tickets, language-specific attachments, and follow-up deadlines — complementing UC-22.2.19's technical cross-border detection.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` (child tasks per country), `nis2_cross_border_incident_register.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **expected_tasks** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where open_tasks > 0 OR tasks_created < expected_tasks` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management by All_Ticket_Management.status, All_Ticket_Management.priority, All_Ticket_Management.category | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**NIS2 Cross-Border Incident Coordination Task Tracking (Art. 23(3))** — When incidents affect multiple Member States, parallel coordination tasks multiply. This use case tracks per-country CSIRT notification tickets, language-specific attachments, and follow-up deadlines — complementing UC-22.2.19's technical cross-border detection.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` (child tasks per country), `nis2_cross_border_incident_register.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: affected_member_states, expected_tasks, incident_id, open_tasks, tasks_created.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (open coordination tasks), Map (Member States), Gantt-style panel (optional), Single value (incidents with open tasks).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for when incidents affect multiple member states, parallel coordination tasks multiply. this use case tracks per-country csirt notification tickets, language-specific attachments, and follow-up deadlin. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
